title: The Borrowed Jacket You Ruined
prompt_text: Last weekend you borrowed your friend Maya's favourite jacket for a party, and you accidentally spilled red wine all over it. The stain will not come out, even after dry cleaning. Call Maya, tell her honestly what happened, apologise sincerely, and explain how you plan to make things right.
vocabulary_tips: take it to the dry cleaner
replace it with a new one
connector_phrases: I have to be honest with you
I'd like to make it up to you
I completely understand if you're upset
template_hint: Greet Maya warmly, signal that the call is hard, explain exactly what happened to the jacket, apologise sincerely, then propose a concrete fix such as buying her an identical replacement, and reassure her the friendship matters more than the jacket.
sample_response_band12: Hey Maya, it's me. Listen, I have to be honest with you about something, and I feel terrible about it. Remember the gorgeous jacket you lent me for Saturday's party? Well, someone bumped into me and I ended up spilling red wine all down the front. I tried everything, and I even took it to the dry cleaner, but the stain just won't come out. I'm so sorry, I know how much you loved it. The thing is, I'd really like to make this right. I've found the exact same one online, so let me buy you a brand-new one, or honestly, whatever you'd prefer. I never want a jacket to come between us. Please tell me what works for you, and again, I'm genuinely sorry. You've always been so generous with me, and I want to fix this properly.

title: The Book You Lost
prompt_text: A few months ago your friend Daniel lent you a book that he treasures because his late grandfather gave it to him. You have searched everywhere, but you cannot find it and you believe it is lost for good. Call Daniel, break the news gently, and discuss how you can make up for losing something so meaningful to him.
connector_phrases: This is really hard to say
I know how much it meant to you
template_hint: Open kindly, warn Daniel you have difficult news, gently explain that the book is lost despite your best efforts, acknowledge its sentimental value, apologise, and offer ways to make amends such as tracking down a copy or another gesture, while reassuring him you value him.
sample_response_band12: Hi Daniel, do you have a minute? This is really hard to say, so I'll just be straight with you. That book you lent me, the one your grandfather gave you, I've searched my whole apartment, my car, everywhere, and I genuinely can't find it. I think it's lost, and I feel awful, because I know exactly how much it meant to you. I'm so sorry. I know I can't truly replace something with that kind of sentimental value. That said, I've started hunting online for the same edition, and I won't stop until I track down a copy for you. If there's anything else I can do to make this right, please just tell me. I should have taken better care of it, and I take full responsibility. Our friendship matters far more to me than being let off the hook.

title: Scratching The Neighbour's Car
prompt_text: While parking this morning, you accidentally scratched the side of your neighbour Mr. Lopez's parked car, leaving a clear mark. Nobody saw it happen, so you could stay quiet, but you have decided to do the right thing. Knock on Mr. Lopez's door, admit what you did, and work out a fair way to cover the repair.
connector_phrases: I want to be upfront with you
template_hint: Greet Mr. Lopez respectfully, explain why you've come, admit clearly that you scratched his car and that it was your fault, apologise, then offer a concrete solution like paying for the repair, and reassure him you'll handle it responsibly.
sample_response_band12: Hi Mr. Lopez, I'm sorry to bother you, but I want to be upfront with you about something. This morning, while I was parking, I accidentally scratched the side of your car. There's a clear mark near the rear door, and it was completely my fault. Nobody else was involved, and I just couldn't walk away without telling you. I'm really sorry. I know how frustrating this is, so here's what I'd like to do. Let me take it to a good auto body shop and cover the full cost of the repair. If you'd rather use your own mechanic, that's absolutely fine too, whatever you think is fair. Please don't worry about a thing on your end; I'll handle all the arrangements. I value being a good neighbour, and I wanted to do the right thing by you.

title: Breaking The Vase At Dinner
prompt_text: At a dinner party last night, you accidentally knocked over and broke an expensive vase at your friend Priya's home. You were so embarrassed that you only mumbled a quick "sorry" before leaving. Call Priya today to apologise properly and decide how you will compensate her for the broken vase.
connector_phrases: I owe you a proper apology
I hope you can forgive me
template_hint: Greet Priya warmly, admit you didn't apologise properly last night, sincerely say sorry for breaking the vase, acknowledge its value, then offer a clear way to compensate her, and close by protecting the friendship.
sample_response_band12: Hi Priya, it's me. I've been thinking about last night, and honestly, I owe you a proper apology. When I knocked over that beautiful vase, I was so embarrassed that I barely said sorry before I rushed out, and that wasn't okay. I feel awful about it, especially because I know it was valuable and probably meant a lot to you. So, first of all, I'm truly sorry. The least I can do is make things right, so I'd really like to either find a replacement that's just as lovely or cover its full cost, whatever you'd prefer. Please be honest with me about what it was worth. The last thing I want is for one clumsy moment to put a shadow over our friendship. I hope you can forgive me, and let's plan another evening soon, my treat.

title: Cracking A Colleague's Laptop
topic: taking responsibility for damaging a colleague's laptop
prompt_text: You borrowed your colleague Sarah's personal laptop to finish a work presentation, and you dropped it, cracking the screen. The laptop still turns on, but the display is badly damaged and will be costly to fix. You can either pay for a screen repair out of your own pocket or offer to buy her a newer model. Speak to Sarah, explain what happened, and tell her which option you've chosen and why.
connector_phrases: I need to tell you something
If it were up to me
Just let me know what works for you
template_hint: Approach Sarah professionally, tell her you have bad news, explain that you dropped and cracked her laptop, take responsibility and apologise, then state which of the two options you've chosen with clear reasons, and reassure her you'll handle it quickly while inviting her input.
sample_response_band12: Sarah, do you have a moment? I need to tell you something, and I'm not proud of it. While I was finishing the presentation on your laptop, I dropped it and cracked the screen. It still turns on, but the display is badly damaged. I take full responsibility, and I'm genuinely sorry, because I know that's your personal machine. I've thought it through, and here's what I'd like to do. Rather than just patching it with a repair, which might leave it weaker than before, I'd rather buy you a newer model so you end up with something reliable. That felt like the fairest option, given it was my mistake. I'll get a quote today and sort it out by the end of the week. Just let me know what specs you'd want, and again, I'm really sorry for the hassle.

title: The Damaged Apartment Wall
prompt_text: While moving a heavy bookshelf in your rented apartment, you accidentally put a large hole in the living-room wall. Your lease requires you to report any damage to your landlord, Mrs. Chen. You can either offer to arrange and pay for a professional repair yourself, or report it and let her organize the fix through her own contractor and bill you afterwards. Call Mrs. Chen, explain what happened, and tell her which option you prefer and why.
connector_phrases: I wanted to let you know right away
I should be upfront with you
Please let me know how you'd like to proceed
template_hint: Greet Mrs. Chen politely, explain you're calling to report damage as the lease requires, describe the hole honestly, then state which repair option you prefer with reasons, offer to cover the cost, and reassure her you're being responsible.
sample_response_band12: Hello Mrs. Chen, this is your tenant from unit four. I wanted to let you know right away that while I was moving a heavy bookshelf today, I accidentally put a large hole in the living-room wall. I should be upfront with you, since the lease asks me to report any damage, and I'd never try to hide something like this. Now, I've thought about the options, and honestly, I'd prefer that you arrange the repair through your own contractor. That way you can be sure the work meets your standards and matches the rest of the apartment. I'm completely happy to cover the full cost, whether you bill me directly or deduct it from my deposit, whatever is easiest for you. I'm really sorry for the inconvenience this causes. Please let me know how you'd like to proceed, and I'll cooperate with whatever you decide.

title: Losing A Coworker's Equipment
prompt_text: Your coworker Tom lent you his professional camera for a weekend trip, and somewhere along the way you lost it. You can either buy him the exact same model, which is expensive, or offer him a slightly cheaper alternative plus some cash. Talk to Tom, explain that the camera is gone, and tell him which option you have decided on and why.
connector_phrases: I feel really bad about this
I want to be fair to you
Does that sound okay to you
template_hint: Approach Tom, admit you have something difficult to share, explain that his camera is lost, apologise and take responsibility, then state your chosen option of the two with clear reasoning, and check that it feels fair to him.
sample_response_band12: Hey Tom, can we talk for a second? I owe you an explanation, and I feel really bad about this. That professional camera you lent me for the weekend, somewhere on the trip I lost track of it, and despite searching everywhere, it's gone. I'm so sorry; I know it was expensive and important to your work. So here's what I'm thinking. Rather than offering you a cheaper substitute and some cash, I want to buy you the exact same model. The reason is simple: you trusted me with your gear, and I want you to be exactly where you started, not worse off. I want to be completely fair to you. I'll place the order this week, so just confirm the model details for me. Does that sound okay to you? Again, I'm truly sorry, and thanks for understanding.

title: The Stain on the Sofa
prompt_text: While visiting your friend Olivia last week, you spilled coffee on her brand-new white sofa and left a noticeable stain. You felt too embarrassed to mention it at the time. Call Olivia, confess what happened, apologise, and offer a fair way to deal with the stained sofa.
connector_phrases: I have a confession to make
I should have said something sooner
template_hint: Greet Olivia warmly, admit you have a confession and should have spoken up sooner, explain that you stained her sofa, apologise, then offer a concrete fix like professional cleaning, and reassure her the friendship is solid.
sample_response_band12: Hi Olivia, it's me. So, I have a confession to make, and I should have said something sooner. When I was over last week, I spilled coffee on your brand-new white sofa and left a stain. I was so embarrassed in the moment that I didn't say anything, and I've felt terrible about it ever since. I'm really sorry, especially since that sofa was brand new. So I'd love to set things right. Let me arrange a professional upholstery cleaning to get the stain out properly, and if it doesn't come clean, I'll cover repairing or replacing the cushion, whatever you'd prefer. Just tell me what works best for you and when's a good time. I value our friendship so much, and I really hope we're okay. Thanks for being so easygoing, and again, I'm sorry I didn't speak up right away.

title: Canceling on Your Friend's Party
prompt_text: Your close friend Daniel has planned a birthday dinner for tonight, and you promised weeks ago that you would be there. Something has come up at the last minute and you can no longer attend. Call Daniel, apologize for canceling so late, explain why you cannot come, and try to keep him from being upset with you.
vocabulary_tips: come up at the last minute
connector_phrases: I feel terrible about this, but
I know this is really bad timing
I promise I'll make it up to you
I was so looking forward to it
Please don't be upset with me
template_hint: Greet Daniel warmly, acknowledge how bad the timing is, explain honestly and briefly why you can't come, apologize sincerely, then offer a concrete way to celebrate together later so the friendship stays intact.
sample_response_band12: Hey Daniel, it's me. Listen, I feel absolutely awful calling you like this, but I'm not going to be able to make it to your dinner tonight, and I am so sorry. The thing is, my manager just called me in for an emergency shift because two people went home sick, and I really can't say no without risking my job. I know this is the worst possible timing, especially after I promised weeks ago that I'd be there, and I hate letting you down on your birthday of all days. Please don't be upset with me. How about this: let me take you out this weekend, just the two of us, my treat, and we'll celebrate properly. I really want to make it up to you. Again, I'm so sorry, and I hope tonight is amazing. Happy birthday, my friend.

title: Stepping Back From The Wedding Party
prompt_text: Months ago you agreed to be a bridesmaid in your friend Priya's wedding, but a serious personal situation now means you can no longer take on the bridal duties. You can either withdraw completely and miss the wedding, or attend as a regular guest with none of the bridesmaid responsibilities. Call Priya, break the news gently, tell her which option you have chosen, and explain your decision in a way that protects your friendship.
connector_phrases: This is really hard for me to say
I never imagined I'd have to do this
I completely understand if you're disappointed
What I'd love to do instead is
It means the world to me that
template_hint: Open gently and signal that the news is hard, acknowledge how much the role meant, explain your situation honestly, clearly state which of the two options you've chosen and why, then reassure Priya of your love and support to preserve the friendship.
sample_response_band12: Priya, hi, do you have a minute? This is really hard for me to say, and I've been dreading this call. I'm so sorry, but I can't be a bridesmaid in your wedding anymore. My mother was just diagnosed with a serious illness, and I'll be flying back and forth to care for her over the next few months, so I honestly can't commit to the fittings, rehearsals, and everything the role involves. I'd never want to leave you scrambling at the last minute. So here's what I'd love to do instead: please let me come as a regular guest, with no duties, so I can still be there for your big day. I completely understand if you're disappointed, and I won't be hurt if you fill the spot. You mean the world to me, and I'll be cheering you on no matter what.

title: Backing Out Of The Move
prompt_text: You agreed to help your friend Marcus move into his new apartment this Saturday, but you've just hurt your back and won't be able to lift anything. Call Marcus, let him know you can't physically help him move, and offer another way to support him so he isn't left stuck without help.
connector_phrases: I really wanted to be there for you
I'd hate to leave you stuck
What I can do instead is
template_hint: Greet your friend, express that you wanted to help, explain the injury and why lifting is impossible, then immediately offer concrete alternatives so he isn't left without support.
sample_response_band12: Hey Marcus, I'm really glad I caught you before Saturday. I've got some bad news, and honestly I feel terrible about it. I threw out my back yesterday, and the doctor told me flat out: no lifting whatsoever for at least two weeks. I really wanted to be there hauling boxes with you, so this is frustrating for me too. The last thing I want is to leave you stuck, so here's what I can do instead. I'll cover half the cost of a couple of movers for the morning, and I'll still come by to direct traffic, order pizza, and keep everyone's spirits up. I can also lend you my car and all my packing supplies. Just tell me what works best for you. I promise I'll make it up to you once my back's healed.

title: Resolving The Double Booking
prompt_text: You accidentally promised two different friends you would spend Friday evening with each of them: a quiet catch-up dinner with Sofia, and a concert with Liam that you agreed to buy tickets for. You can only do one, so you must cancel on the other. Decide which friend to keep your plans with, then call the friend you are canceling on to explain your decision and smooth things over.
connector_phrases: I feel really bad about this
Can I take a rain check
template_hint: Apologize right away, honestly admit the scheduling mix-up, explain why you're keeping the other plan, then propose a specific make-up plan and reassure the friend you're canceling on.
sample_response_band12: Hi Sofia, I owe you a big apology, and I wanted to call rather than text. I completely double-booked myself for Friday, and I'm so embarrassed. What happened was, I already promised Liam ages ago that I'd buy the concert tickets and go with him, and they're non-refundable, so I'm locked in. When we made our dinner plans, it totally slipped my mind, and that's on me. I really hate canceling on you, because honestly I'd much rather have a quiet catch-up than fight a crowd. So can I take a rain check? Let me treat you to dinner next week, wherever you want, my treat, with the whole evening to ourselves and no rush. I feel terrible about the mix-up, and I really hope we're okay. Thanks for being so understanding, Sofia.

title: Pulling Out Of The Group Trip
prompt_text: Months ago you committed to a group road trip with several friends, and your friend Aisha is organizing everything and counting on your share of the costs. Your finances have changed and you can no longer afford to go as planned. You can either back out of the trip entirely, or join only for a cheaper part of it. Call Aisha, decide which option you will take, and explain your decision honestly while keeping things on good terms with the group.
connector_phrases: I hate to do this to the group
I don't want to leave everyone short
I really appreciate all your planning
template_hint: Greet the organizer, acknowledge the planning she's done, explain your changed finances honestly, state clearly which option you've chosen, and offer to soften the impact so the group isn't left short.
sample_response_band12: Hey Aisha, thanks for picking up. I hate making this call, because I know how much work you've put into organizing this trip. The honest truth is, my hours got cut at work and money is really tight right now, so I can't commit to the full trip the way I promised, and I feel awful about it. I don't want to leave everyone short or mess up the budget. So here's what I'm hoping: instead of dropping out completely, would it be possible for me to join just for the weekend leg, the cheaper part, and skip the longer week? That way I'm still part of it without breaking the bank. If that complicates the planning, I totally understand, and I'll cover any deposit I've already cost the group. I really appreciate everything you've done, and I'd love to still be there in some way.

title: Postponing The Family Visit
prompt_text: You have planned for a long time to visit your aunt Carol, who lives far away and has been excited for weeks to see you. A sudden work deadline now makes the trip impossible. Call your aunt to gently call off the visit, explain why, and reassure her that you still want to see her soon so she is not too disappointed.
connector_phrases: I've been so excited to see you
I'm so sorry to do this
template_hint: Open warmly with affection, acknowledge how much the visit means to both of you, explain the work conflict, then propose a new date and reassure her the relationship comes first.
sample_response_band12: Hi Auntie Carol, it's so good to hear your voice. Listen, I have to tell you something, and it's breaking my heart a little. I've been counting down the days to come see you, but I'm so sorry, I have to call off the trip this time. The problem is, a huge project at work got moved up, and the deadline lands right in the middle of when I was supposed to be there. Please know this isn't goodbye, it's just a delay. How about I come the long weekend next month instead, and stay a few extra days to make up for it? I'll even cook your favourite dinner when I'm there. I love you so much, and I promise I'll make this happen soon. Give my love to everyone, okay?

title: Missing A Coworker's Farewell
prompt_text: You already RSVP'd yes to a farewell party for your coworker Ben, who is leaving the company on Friday, but a family emergency now means you cannot attend. Call Ben directly, let him know you will not be able to make his send-off, and find a way to say a proper goodbye so he does not think you did not care.
connector_phrases: I really didn't want to miss this
I'd hate for you to think
I wish you all the best
template_hint: Greet your coworker, express that you genuinely wanted to be there, explain the emergency, then arrange an alternative goodbye and offer warm well-wishes so the relationship ends on a high note.
sample_response_band12: Hi Ben, I'm glad I caught you. I really wanted to tell you this myself rather than just not showing up. I RSVP'd yes to your farewell on Friday and I was genuinely looking forward to it, but a family emergency has come up and I have to travel out of town that day. I'd hate for you to think I didn't care, because honestly, working with you has been one of the best parts of this job. So before you go, can I take you for a quick coffee tomorrow morning, just the two of us, so I can give you a proper send-off? And let's definitely stay in touch afterward; I'll add you on LinkedIn today. I'm so sorry to miss the party, but I'm truly happy for you. Congratulations on the new role, and all the very best, Ben.

title: Dropping Your Volunteer Shift
prompt_text: You signed up to volunteer at a community food bank this Saturday, but you've come down with a contagious illness and shouldn't be around others while handling food. Call the volunteer coordinator, Mr. Tanaka, and explain that you have to pull out of your shift. Apologize for the short notice and try to leave the team in a good position despite the late change.
connector_phrases: I'm really sorry for the short notice
I wouldn't want to put anyone at risk
Is there anything I can do
template_hint: Greet the coordinator politely, apologize for the short notice, explain the illness and why it's safer to stay home, then offer to help find a replacement and recommit for the future.
sample_response_band12: Hi Mr. Tanaka, I'm so sorry to call on such short notice. Unfortunately, I have to pull out of my volunteer shift this Saturday at the food bank. I've come down with the flu, and since it's contagious and I'd be handling food and standing close to people, I really don't think it's safe for me to be there. The last thing I'd want is to get the team or our clients sick. I know Saturdays are busy and I'd hate to leave you shorthanded, so if it helps, I'd be happy to message the other volunteers to see if anyone can cover my slot, and I can email you a few reliable names. Please let me know what would be most useful. And the moment I'm better, I'll sign up for a double shift to make up for it. Again, I'm really sorry, and thank you for understanding.

title: Saying No To A Loan
prompt_text: A close friend has just called to ask you to lend them a large sum of money to cover an unexpected bill. You care about this person, but lending that amount would put your own finances and your friendship at real risk. Call your friend back and explain why you have to say no, while keeping the relationship strong and offering some other kind of help.
connector_phrases: I want to be honest with you
I completely understand if you're disappointed
What I can do instead is
template_hint: Greet your friend warmly, acknowledge that the request is hard to bring up, explain your own financial limits, clearly say no with your reasons, offer an alternative form of help, and reassure them the friendship matters.
sample_response_band12: Hey Maya, thanks for trusting me enough to call about this. I can hear how stressful that bill is, and I really wish I could just hand you the money. Honestly, though, I have to be straight with you. After my own bills and a couple of unexpected expenses this month, I'm financially stretched, and lending that much would put me in a tough spot too. So as much as it pains me, I can't lend you that amount. I completely understand if you're disappointed, and I hope you know it's not about not caring. What I can do is help you look at your options. I could go over your budget with you tonight, or help you call the company about a payment plan. I really value our friendship, and I want to support you through this in a way that actually works for both of us.

title: Declining To Lend Your Car
prompt_text: Your cousin has asked to borrow your car for the weekend to go on a road trip. You depend on the car yourself and you are not comfortable with someone else driving it that far. Speak to your cousin, politely decline the request, explain your reasons, and suggest another way they could still make their trip work.
connector_phrases: It's not that I don't trust you
template_hint: Open warmly with your cousin and say the trip sounds fun, explain why you need the car and your concerns, clearly but kindly decline, then suggest a practical alternative and reassure them it is not personal.
sample_response_band12: Hey Daniel, I really appreciate you thinking of me, and that road trip honestly sounds amazing. I want to be upfront with you, though. The truth is I rely on my car all week for work and errands, so I'd be completely stuck without it that whole weekend. On top of that, a long trip adds a lot of wear and tear, and there's the insurance side too, which makes me a bit uneasy. So I'm really sorry, but I'm not going to be able to lend it to you this time. It's honestly not that I don't trust your driving. Would it help if I looked into a weekend car rental with you? The deals can be surprisingly cheap, and I'd be happy to split the booking online. I really hope you understand, and I'd love to hear all about the trip when you get back.

title: Refusing to Cover a Shift
prompt_text: A coworker has asked you to cover their shift this Saturday, but you already have important plans you can't change. You want to stay on good terms with this colleague, who has helped you in the past. Speak to your coworker and explain why you can't take the shift, while showing you're still a supportive teammate.
connector_phrases: I know you'd do the same for me
template_hint: Greet your coworker, recognize that they're in a bind and that they've helped you before, explain your unchangeable plans, clearly decline, suggest who else might help or another way to support them, and reaffirm your teamwork.
sample_response_band12: Hi Priya, I'm really glad you came to me, and I know you've covered for me before, so I genuinely wish I could return the favour this time. Unfortunately, this Saturday just doesn't work for me. I already committed to a family event months ago, and it's something I really can't move. So as much as I hate to, I have to say I can't take your shift. I know that puts you in a tough spot, and I'm sorry about that. What I can do is help you find someone. I think Marco mentioned he wanted extra hours, so I'd be happy to message the team chat and ask around for you right now. And if you ever need someone for a weekday shift, I'm your person. I really do value working with you, and I want to make sure you're covered.

title: Declining A Party Invitation
prompt_text: A close friend has invited you to their big birthday party this weekend, but you are completely exhausted and know you need to rest. You do not want your friend to feel hurt or think you do not care about them. Call your friend, let them know you cannot make it, and explain honestly while making sure they know the friendship matters to you.
connector_phrases: I was really looking forward to it
I'd hate for you to think
I promise I'll make it up to you
template_hint: Open warmly and thank your friend for the invite, acknowledge the party is important to them, explain honestly why you can't come, clearly decline, offer to celebrate another way, and reassure them you care.
sample_response_band12: Hey Sofia, first of all, happy early birthday, and thank you so much for inviting me. I was genuinely looking forward to celebrating with you. I have to be honest, though. This week has completely wiped me out, and if I push myself to come on Saturday, I'll be a mess and probably make myself sick. So as much as it hurts to say it, I'm not going to be able to make it to the party. I'd hate for you to think I don't care, because I really do. So how about this. Can we do a proper birthday brunch, just the two of us, next weekend? My treat, anywhere you want. I promise I'll make it up to you. You mean a lot to me, and I really hope you have the most amazing night surrounded by everyone who loves you.

title: Turning Down A Babysitting Request
prompt_text: Your next-door neighbour has asked you to babysit their young children on a regular basis, but you don't feel comfortable taking on that responsibility and you don't have the time. You want to stay on friendly terms since you live right beside each other. Speak to your neighbour, decline the request politely, explain your reasons clearly, and suggest another option that could help them.
connector_phrases: That's really kind of you to ask
I just don't think I'd be able to
I wouldn't want to let you down
template_hint: Greet your neighbour warmly, acknowledge they need reliable help, explain honestly why you can't commit, clearly decline the regular arrangement, suggest a practical alternative like a sitting service or another neighbour, and keep the door open for small future favours.
sample_response_band12: Hi Karen, thanks so much for thinking of me, and it's really sweet that you'd trust me with your kids. I want to be honest with you, though, because you deserve someone reliable. Between my work schedule and a few other commitments, I'm honestly stretched too thin right now, so I just don't think I'd be able to babysit on a regular basis the way you need. I'd hate to say yes and then keep letting you down at the last minute, because that wouldn't be fair to you. So I'm really sorry, but I have to say no to the regular arrangement. Have you thought about the sitting service the family on the corner uses? I've heard great things, and I'm happy to grab the number for you. And if you're ever stuck for an hour in an emergency, just knock on my door.

title: Telling Parents You're Moving Abroad
prompt_text: You have decided to make a major change in your life that your parents will not be happy about, such as moving to another country or switching to a completely different career. You know they have always hoped you would stay close and follow a more traditional path. Speak to one of your parents. Explain your decision calmly and reassure them so that the conversation stays peaceful.
connector_phrases: I know this might be hard to hear, but
Please hear me out before you react
I've thought about this for a long time
I understand why you'd worry, and
This doesn't change how much I love you
I promise we'll stay close no matter what
template_hint: Open with love and a gentle warning, explain the decision and your reasoning, acknowledge their fears, then reassure them the bond stays strong.
sample_response_band12: Mom, can we sit down for a minute? There's something important I need to share, and I know it might be hard to hear. I've decided to take a job abroad, and I'll be moving overseas in a couple of months. I know you've always hoped I'd stay close, and believe me, I didn't make this choice lightly. I weighed everything carefully, and this is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity that I just can't pass up. I understand you're worried, and that means the world to me. But please trust that I'm at peace with this. I promise to call every week, visit on holidays, and fly you out to see my new place. Nothing about the distance changes how much I love you and Dad. You raised me to chase my dreams, and that's exactly what I'm doing. Can we figure this out together?

title: Saying No To Co-Signing
prompt_text: A relative has asked you to co-sign a loan for them, which would make you legally responsible for the debt if they ever fail to repay it. You are not comfortable taking on that financial liability, but this is family and you do not want to cause tension. Speak directly to your relative, explain warmly why you have to decline, and show that you still want to help them find another solution.
connector_phrases: I know this isn't easy to talk about
It's not a reflection of how I feel about you
What I'd be willing to do is
template_hint: Greet your relative warmly, acknowledge the situation is delicate, explain that co-signing makes you legally liable and why that worries you, clearly decline, offer a safer way to help, and reassure them that family comes first.
sample_response_band12: Hi Uncle Ravi, I know this isn't an easy thing to bring up, and I really appreciate that you trusted me enough to ask. I want to be completely honest with you, though. The reality is that co-signing would make me legally responsible for the whole loan if anything ever went wrong, and that's a level of risk I just wouldn't be comfortable taking on, especially with my own family to think about. So as hard as it is, I have to say I can't co-sign. Please don't read this as me doubting you, because it really isn't about that. What I'd be willing to do is sit down with you, look at credit unions that might not need a co-signer, and help you build a stronger application. We're family, and I'm not going anywhere. Let's figure this out together, okay?

title: Pointing Out Errors In A Report
prompt_text: You and a coworker named Daniel worked together on a project, and Daniel wrote the final report. While reviewing it, you noticed that it contains serious errors in the numbers and a few incorrect conclusions, and it is due to your manager tomorrow morning. Talk to Daniel in person, point out the problems honestly but kindly, and suggest how the two of you can fix them before the deadline.
connector_phrases: I really appreciate the work you put into this, but
I noticed a couple of things that don't quite add up
I might be wrong, but it looks like
The thing is, it's due tomorrow
Maybe we could go through it together
I'm sure we can get this sorted out
template_hint: Address the coworker warmly and acknowledge their effort, then gently raise the specific errors with evidence, explain why they matter given the deadline, propose a concrete plan to fix them together, and reassure them you're on their side.
sample_response_band12: Hey Daniel, do you have a minute? First off, I really appreciate how much work you put into this report, it reads really well. There's something I wanted to flag, though, and I hope you don't take it the wrong way. When I was reviewing it, I noticed a few of the figures in the budget section don't quite add up, and a couple of the conclusions seem to be based on those numbers. The thing is, it's going to the manager first thing tomorrow, so I think we need to get it right tonight. How about we sit down together and go through the data line by line? I can double-check the spreadsheet while you tweak the wording. Honestly, with both of us on it, I'm confident we can have it polished within an hour. We've got this.

title: Asking A Coworker To Quiet Down
prompt_text: You sit next to a coworker named Priya, whom you like and get along with well. Throughout the day she taps her pen loudly and plays music without headphones, and the noise is making it hard for you to concentrate. You don't want to embarrass her or seem unfriendly, but you need things to be quieter. Talk to Priya directly and politely ask her to change the distracting habit.
connector_phrases: I hope this doesn't come across the wrong way
I've been finding it a little hard to focus
Maybe we could find a middle ground
template_hint: Open in a friendly way and reassure her it's nothing personal, describe the noise and how it affects your focus, make a clear and polite request, offer an easy compromise such as headphones, and thank her warmly to keep things relaxed.
sample_response_band12: Hey Priya, can I grab you for a second? I hope this doesn't come across the wrong way, because I really do enjoy sitting next to you. The thing is, I've been finding it a bit hard to concentrate lately, and I think part of it is the music playing out loud and the pen tapping, especially around deadlines. It's honestly nothing personal, I'm just someone who needs it pretty quiet to focus. So I was wondering, would you mind popping your headphones in when you've got music on? And maybe easing up on the tapping when I'm in the zone? I totally get that everyone works differently, so if I ever do anything noisy that bugs you, please tell me too. Maybe we can find a middle ground. Thanks so much for understanding.

title: Addressing An Unequal Workload
prompt_text: You are part of a small team finishing an important project, and one teammate named Marcus has been doing very little while the rest of you carry the workload. The deadline is approaching and the imbalance is causing tension within the group. Talk to Marcus directly: explain that he isn't pulling his weight and agree on a fair way for him to contribute, while keeping your working relationship intact.
connector_phrases: I wanted to talk to you one-on-one
I don't want to put you on the spot, but
Is everything okay on your end?
Let's figure out a way forward
template_hint: Speak to him privately and set a calm tone, describe the imbalance using facts rather than blame, give him space to explain in case something is going on, clearly state what you need from him, and agree on a fair division of the remaining tasks.
sample_response_band12: Hey Marcus, do you have a minute to chat one-on-one? I wanted to bring something up directly rather than let it build up. From where I'm standing, it feels like the rest of us have been carrying most of the project lately, and with the deadline approaching, things are getting a bit tense. I don't want to put you on the spot, so first, is everything okay on your end? If something's going on, I completely get it. But honestly, what I'm hoping is that we can split the remaining work more evenly. How about you take the research section and the slides, and we'll handle the rest? That way nobody feels stretched too thin. I really value working with you, Marcus, and I think if we sort this out now, we'll cross the finish line strong together. Does that sound fair to you?

title: Asking Relatives To Stop Dropping By
prompt_text: Your in-laws live nearby and often show up at your home without calling first, sometimes at inconvenient times. You value the relationship, but the surprise visits are starting to disrupt your family's routine and privacy. Speak to one of your in-laws. Kindly ask them to let you know before they come over, without hurting their feelings.
vocabulary_tips: catch us at a good time
connector_phrases: I really appreciate how close we are, and
I hope this comes across the right way
Would you mind just texting first
That way we can give you our full attention
We'd love to see you even more this way
template_hint: Open by valuing their visits, gently raise the issue of timing, make a small specific request, and frame it as wanting better visits, not fewer.
sample_response_band12: Hi Linda, do you have a second? I wanted to chat about something, and I hope it comes across the right way. First, I want you to know how much we love having you in our lives, and the kids absolutely adore their grandma. Here's the thing, though. With our work schedules and the kids' activities, our days can get pretty hectic, and sometimes a surprise visit catches us at a really chaotic moment. Would you mind giving us a quick call or text before you head over? It's honestly nothing personal at all. That way we can make sure we're home, the place is tidy, and we can actually sit down and enjoy your company instead of rushing around. We'd genuinely love to see you even more often this way. Does that sound okay to you?

title: Asking To Stop Being Interrupted
prompt_text: In team meetings, a colleague named Tom often cuts you off before you can finish your ideas, and lately it has started to frustrate you. You want to address it so you can be heard, but Tom is generally well-meaning and you don't want a confrontation. Talk to Tom privately, explain how the interruptions affect you, and ask him to let you finish your points.
connector_phrases: I wanted to mention something between us
I know you don't mean anything by it
It would mean a lot if
I really value your input too
template_hint: Pull him aside privately and keep it low-key, assume good intent so he isn't defensive, describe the interruptions and how they make you feel, make a simple specific request, and reassure him you respect his contributions.
sample_response_band12: Hey Tom, can I grab you for a sec? I wanted to mention something just between us, nothing serious. I know you don't mean anything by it, and honestly you're one of the most enthusiastic people on the team. But I've noticed that lately, in our meetings, I sometimes get cut off before I can finish a thought, and I'll be honest, it's left me feeling a bit unheard. All I'm asking is, if you could let me wrap up my point before jumping in, it would really mean a lot to me. I'd happily do the same for you, of course, I really value your input. I just want us both to have the floor. Thanks for hearing me out, Tom, I appreciate you not taking it the wrong way.

title: Asking A Coworker To Return Tools
prompt_text: A few weeks ago you lent some of your tools to Ben, a coworker you get along with well. He still hasn't returned them, and now you need them for a project of your own. Talk to Ben directly: remind him about the tools and ask him to return them soon, while keeping things friendly and not sounding like you are accusing him.
connector_phrases: I hope you don't mind me asking
It's just that I need them for
template_hint: Greet Ben casually and bring it up lightly, gently remind him about the specific tools, explain why you need them back now, suggest an easy time and way to hand them over, and keep the tone warm so he doesn't feel scolded.
sample_response_band12: Hey Ben, how's it going? I hope you don't mind me asking, but do you remember those tools I lent you a few weeks back, the drill and the set of wrenches? No big deal at all, but I was just wondering if you could get them back to me this week. It's just that I've got a project of my own starting this weekend, and I could really use them. Whenever works for you, honestly. You could drop them by my desk tomorrow, or I'm happy to swing by your place and grab them if that's easier. I figured it just slipped your mind, it happens to everyone. And I'm glad they came in handy for whatever you were working on. Anyway, thanks a million, Ben, I really appreciate it. Just let me know what works best for you.

title: Talking About A Hurtful Comment
prompt_text: During a recent team conversation, your coworker Rachel made a comment about your work in front of others that genuinely upset you, though you suspect she didn't realize how it came across. You want to clear the air so it doesn't damage your relationship going forward. Talk to Rachel privately, tell her how the comment made you feel, and ask to move past it on good terms.
connector_phrases: I wanted to clear the air with you
I'm sure you didn't mean it that way, but
It kind of caught me off guard
I just wanted you to know
I'd really like to put this behind us
template_hint: Approach her privately and frame it as clearing the air, give her the benefit of the doubt, describe the specific comment and use I statements to share how it felt, invite her perspective, and end by affirming you want to move forward on good terms.
sample_response_band12: Hey Rachel, do you have a minute? There's something I wanted to clear the air about, just the two of us. The other day, when we were talking in front of the team, you made a comment about my work, and I'm sure you didn't mean it the way it landed. But to be honest, it kind of caught me off guard, and I took it a little to heart. I'm not telling you this to make you feel bad. I just wanted you to know how it came across so it doesn't sit between us. Maybe I read it wrong, and if so, that's okay too. I'd love to hear your side. At the end of the day, I really value working with you, so I'd rather we talk it through than let it linger. No hard feelings. Can we just put it behind us?

title: Missing An Important Deadline
prompt_text: You have been working on a major report that is due tomorrow, but you have realized it is impossible to finish it on time and still meet the quality your boss expects. You can either submit a rushed, incomplete version by the deadline, or ask your boss for two more days to deliver it properly. Decide which option you will choose and explain your decision to your boss, who is counting on this report for an important client meeting.
connector_phrases: I'm really sorry to bring this up, but
I want to be upfront with you
I promise I'll have it ready by
template_hint: Greet your boss and signal there's a problem, acknowledge how important the report is, honestly explain why you can't finish on time, state which option you've chosen with clear reasons, offer a specific solution or timeline, and reassure them you'll deliver quality work.
sample_response_band12: Hi Mark, do you have a minute? I need to be honest with you about the quarterly report. I've been working flat out on it, but the data took much longer to verify than I expected, and the truth is it won't be ready by tomorrow morning if I want it to be accurate. I know how much is riding on this for the client meeting, so the last thing I want is to hand you something rushed with mistakes in it. Rather than rushing it, what I'd like to propose is this: give me two extra days and I'll deliver a polished, fully checked version you can present with confidence. In the meantime, I can send you a one-page summary of the key figures so you're not walking in empty-handed. I'm really sorry for the inconvenience, and I promise this won't become a habit. Does that work for you?

title: Asking To Cut Overtime Hours
prompt_text: For the past two months you have been working overtime almost every evening, and it is starting to affect your health and your family life. You need to ask your manager to reduce your overtime so you can return to a normal schedule, even though the team is busy and your manager relies on you heavily. Speak to your manager, explain your situation, and ask them to cut back your overtime hours while keeping a good working relationship.
template_hint: Open by thanking your manager and acknowledging the team is busy, gently raise that the overtime is taking a toll, explain the impact on your health and family, request a return to normal hours with reasons, suggest how the work could still get done, and reaffirm your commitment to the team.
sample_response_band12: Hi Sandra, thanks for making time for me. First, I want you to know I really value being part of this team, and I've been happy to put in extra hours while we've been short-staffed. But I have to be honest with you: I've been doing overtime almost every night for two months now, and it's really catching up with me. I'm exhausted, I'm barely seeing my kids, and I'm worried that if I keep this up, my work quality will start to slip. So what I'm asking is whether we can scale my hours back to a normal schedule going forward. I'm not stepping back from my responsibilities at all. Maybe we could look at redistributing a few tasks, or I'd be glad to help train someone to share the load. I just need this to be sustainable. Could we work something out together?

title: Declining An Extra Assignment
prompt_text: Your manager has just offered you an extra assignment, but your schedule is already completely full and taking it on would mean letting your current projects suffer. You can either accept the assignment to look like a team player, or politely decline so you can protect the quality of your existing work. Decide which option you will choose and explain your decision to your manager, who clearly wants you to say yes.
connector_phrases: I'm flattered that you thought of me
Right now my plate is full
I'd hate to let you down by
template_hint: Thank your manager for thinking of you, acknowledge the assignment matters, honestly explain that your current workload is full, state your decision to decline with reasons, offer an alternative such as a later date or another colleague, and reassure them you still want to contribute.
sample_response_band12: Hi David, thanks so much for thinking of me for the new marketing project. I'm genuinely flattered, and I can tell it's important to you. But I want to be straight with you rather than overpromise. Right now my plate is completely full with the budget review and the client onboarding, and both of those have tight deadlines this month. If I took on a third major task, I honestly couldn't do justice to any of them, and I'd hate for the quality to slip on work you're already counting on. So as much as I'd love to jump in, I think the responsible thing is to pass on this one for now. That said, if it can wait until next month, I'd be glad to take the lead then. Or I'm happy to suggest a colleague who has the capacity right now. Would either of those work?

title: Handing In Your Resignation
prompt_text: After much thought, you have accepted a new job and you must now tell your manager that you are resigning from your current position. Your manager has been supportive and a good mentor, so you want to leave on excellent terms without burning any bridges. Speak to your manager, deliver the news that you are resigning, and explain your decision in a way that shows respect and gratitude.
connector_phrases: This isn't an easy thing to say
I can't thank you enough for
Please know that this isn't about
I'd like to leave on the best possible terms
template_hint: Ask to speak privately and signal the news is significant, express how hard the decision was, deliver the resignation clearly, explain the reason while emphasizing gratitude for their mentorship, offer to ensure a smooth handover, and end by reaffirming respect and goodwill.
sample_response_band12: Sarah, do you have a moment? There's something I need to tell you, and honestly, it isn't easy. After a lot of thought, I've decided to accept a new opportunity, so I'm handing in my notice today. Please know this has nothing to do with the team or with you. You've been one of the best managers I've ever had, and so much of what I've learned here is thanks to your mentorship. This was genuinely a bittersweet decision, because I care a lot about the people here. The new role is just a step I felt I had to take for my long-term growth. I want to make this transition as smooth as possible, so I'll give a full handover, document everything, and help train whoever takes over. I really hope we can stay in touch, and I'll always be grateful for the chance you gave me here.

title: Stepping Back From A Project
prompt_text: You were assigned to a technical project that has turned out to be far beyond your current skills, and despite your best efforts you keep falling behind and feeling overwhelmed. You can either keep struggling on alone and risk failing, or ask your manager to take you off the project and reassign you to work that suits your strengths. Decide which option you will choose and explain your decision to your manager, who originally trusted you with this project.
connector_phrases: I've been wrestling with this for a while
I have to be honest with myself and with you
I don't want to let the team down by
What I think would be best is
template_hint: Open honestly and acknowledge the project matters, explain that the work is beyond your current skills despite real effort, own the difficulty without making excuses, request to be reassigned with clear reasoning, suggest a smooth handover or a better-suited role, and reassure your manager of your commitment.
sample_response_band12: Hi Tom, thanks for sitting down with me. I've been wrestling with this for a couple of weeks, and I have to be honest with you. The database migration project has turned out to be well beyond my current skill set. I've put in long hours and tried everything, but I keep falling behind, and I'm worried I could put the whole timeline at risk. I really appreciate that you trusted me with it, which is exactly why I don't want to let you down by struggling on in silence. So what I'd like to ask is whether you could reassign it to someone with more technical experience and put me on work that plays to my strengths. I'm happy to document everything I've done so the handover is smooth and to support whoever takes over. I want this project to succeed. Would that be okay?

title: Disputing The New Shift Schedule
prompt_text: Your manager has just posted a new shift schedule that puts you on late-night and weekend shifts you cannot work because of childcare and transport problems. Even though your manager spent a lot of time creating it and your coworkers have already accepted their shifts, you need to speak to your manager, explain why the new schedule does not work for you, and ask for an adjustment without sounding ungrateful or difficult.
connector_phrases: I know you put a lot of work into this
I hate to be the one to raise this, but
The problem on my end is
Would there be any way to
I really want to make this work for both of us
template_hint: Acknowledge the effort your manager put into the schedule, politely raise that there's a problem for you, clearly explain the childcare and transport conflicts, request a specific change with reasons, offer a flexible compromise such as swapping shifts, and close by emphasizing you want a solution that works for everyone.
sample_response_band12: Hi Lisa, I know you put a lot of work into the new schedule, and I really don't want to seem difficult, but I have to flag a problem on my end. The new roster has me on late-night and weekend shifts, and unfortunately I just can't make those work. My childcare only covers daytime hours, and there's no public transport when those late shifts finish, so I'd have no safe way home. I completely understand you can't rebuild everything around one person, so I want to be flexible too. Would there be any way to keep me on daytime shifts? I'd happily swap with anyone who actually prefers nights and weekends, and I can pick up extra daytime hours to make up for it. I really want to find something that works for both of us. Could we go through the options together?

title: Admitting A Costly Work Mistake
prompt_text: You have just realized that a mistake you made has cost the company a significant amount of money, and your boss does not yet know about it. You can either go to your boss right away, admit the error, and propose a fix, or quietly try to correct it yourself first and only report it if it cannot be resolved. Decide which option you will choose and explain your decision to your boss, taking responsibility while trying to keep their trust.
connector_phrases: There's something I need to come clean about
Here's what I'd like to do to fix it
I completely understand if you're upset
I promise this won't happen again
template_hint: Ask to speak privately and signal the matter is serious, admit the mistake clearly without excuses, briefly explain how it happened, state that you are taking full responsibility, say why you chose to come forward now (or to attempt a fix first), propose a concrete plan to fix it and prevent a repeat, and close by acknowledging the impact and asking how to make it right.
sample_response_band12: Mr. Chen, do you have a few minutes? There's something I need to come clean about, and I'd rather you hear it directly from me. While processing last week's supplier order, I entered the wrong quantity, and that oversight has cost us around five thousand dollars in excess stock. I want to be clear: this was my error, and I take full responsibility. I won't make excuses. I thought about quietly fixing it myself, but I decided you needed to know now so we can limit the damage together. Here's what I'd like to do: I've already contacted the supplier about returning part of the order, and I think we can recover most of the cost. Going forward, I'll add a second check to my process so this can't happen again. I completely understand if you're upset. I just want to make this right. How would you like me to proceed?

title: Hotel Room Not As Booked
prompt_text: You arrive at a hotel after a long trip, but the room you are given is not the one you reserved online. You booked a quiet, non-smoking king room with a city view, yet you received a smoky room with two small beds facing a parking lot. Go to the front desk and speak to the receptionist. Explain the problem clearly and ask them to fix it.
connector_phrases: I'm sorry to bother you, but
template_hint: Greet the receptionist politely, acknowledge they may be busy, explain exactly how the room differs from your booking with evidence, state what you need (the correct room or an equivalent upgrade), and close by thanking them for sorting it out quickly.
sample_response_band12: Hi there, sorry to bother you at the end of a long day. I just checked into room 214, but I think there's been a mix-up with my reservation. According to my booking confirmation, I reserved a non-smoking king room with a city view, but the room I was given has two small beds, smells strongly of smoke, and faces the parking lot. I've been travelling all day, so I was really hoping for the quiet room I actually paid for. Would it be possible to move me into the room I booked, or, if that's full, something equivalent? I understand things get hectic this time of night, and I'm not trying to make your evening harder. I just want what's on my confirmation. I'd genuinely appreciate it. Thank you so much for helping me get this sorted out tonight.

title: Poor Service At A Restaurant
prompt_text: You went out for an important dinner, but the service was very poor: your order took over an hour, the food arrived cold, and your server was rude when you asked for help. Before you leave, you decide to speak privately with the restaurant manager. Explain what happened during your visit and tell the manager what you would like them to do about it.
connector_phrases: Could I have a quick word?
I have to be honest with you,
I'm not usually one to complain, but
template_hint: Ask for a private moment with the manager, calmly acknowledge you don't enjoy complaining, lay out the specific problems in order, explain why it mattered tonight, state what you want them to do, and end on a note that leaves the door open for a return.
sample_response_band12: Excuse me, could I have a quick word before we go? I have to be honest with you, tonight didn't go well, and I wanted to mention it to you directly rather than just leaving upset. We waited over an hour for our food, and when it finally came, the plates were cold. What really bothered me, though, was that when I politely asked our server for help, he was quite short with us. This was meant to be a special dinner, so it stung a little. I'm not usually one to complain, but I'd hope you can understand why I'm disappointed. I'm not asking for anything dramatic, maybe just look into the service tonight and consider adjusting our bill. I've heard good things about this place, and honestly, I'd love a reason to come back. Thanks for hearing me out.

title: Disputing An Incorrect Bill Charge
prompt_text: You are reviewing your monthly phone bill and notice a charge for a premium service you never signed up for, as well as a late fee even though you paid on time. The amount is significant, and your previous attempts to fix it online have failed. Call the company's customer service representative, explain the errors on your bill, and ask them to remove the incorrect charges.
connector_phrases: I'd like to ask you to
I'm sure we can sort this out
template_hint: State why you're calling and identify your account, walk through each incorrect charge with the facts, mention your earlier failed attempts so they aren't repeated, ask clearly for the charges to be reversed, and stay firm but cooperative to reach a resolution.
sample_response_band12: Hi, I'm calling about my latest phone bill, and I'm hoping you can help me clear up a couple of charges. Here's the issue, there's a fee for a premium service I genuinely never signed up for, and on top of that, I've been hit with a late fee even though I paid the full amount on time. To be clear, I have the payment confirmation right in front of me. I want to be upfront, I've already tried fixing this through your website twice and nothing changed, which is why I'm calling now. I'd like to ask you to reverse both charges, the premium fee and the late fee, since neither one is valid. I know billing mistakes happen and it's probably not your fault personally, so I'm not upset with you. I'm sure we can sort this out together. What information do you need from me to get started?

title: Refund For A Disappointing Tour
prompt_text: You paid a premium price for a guided city tour that promised three major attractions, a knowledgeable guide, and a small group. Instead, the bus was overcrowded, one attraction was skipped, and the guide barely spoke English. You contact the tour company afterward to sort it out. Speak to the agent, explain how the tour failed to match what was advertised, and request a refund or fair compensation.
vocabulary_tips: to live up to the promise
connector_phrases: I wanted to follow up about
I think it's only fair that
I'd hate to leave a bad review, but
template_hint: Identify which tour and date you're calling about, acknowledge the guide may have tried their best, contrast the promises with what actually happened point by point, state your request for a refund or compensation with clear reasoning, and signal goodwill so they're motivated to help.
sample_response_band12: Hi, I wanted to follow up about the city tour I took with you last Saturday, the premium one. To be honest with you, it really didn't live up to what was promised. The listing said a small group with a knowledgeable, English-speaking guide and three major attractions. In reality, the bus was packed, we completely skipped one attraction, and our guide could barely communicate with us in English. I paid extra specifically for that small-group experience, so I felt quite let down. Given all that, I think it's only fair to ask for a refund, or at least meaningful compensation for the part we didn't receive. I'm not trying to be difficult, and I'd genuinely rather resolve this with you directly than leave a negative review online. So what can we do to make this right? I'd really appreciate your help.

title: Badly Done Repair Job
prompt_text: You hired a company to repair a leak under your kitchen sink, and you paid in full for the work. A week later the same pipe is leaking again, and there is now water damage to the cabinet below. Call the repair company's representative and explain that the job was not done properly. Ask them to send someone back to fix it correctly at no extra cost, and raise the issue of the cabinet damage.
vocabulary_tips: to follow up on the repair
connector_phrases: I'm calling to follow up about
template_hint: Say why you're calling and reference the original job, describe how the repair failed and the resulting cabinet damage, remind them you paid in full and that you'd assumed/expected the workmanship to hold up, acknowledge the technician may have been rushed rather than blaming him, request a free return visit, raise the cabinet damage, and frame fixing it as protecting their reputation too.
sample_response_band12: Hi, I'm calling to follow up about the leak repair your team did under my kitchen sink last week. Unfortunately, the same pipe has started leaking again, and now there's water damage to the cabinet underneath. The problem is, I paid the full amount for that job, and I'd assumed the fix would last. I'm sure you'd agree that a week is far too soon for the same leak to come back. I'm not blaming the technician personally, maybe he was rushed that day, but I'd really expect the work to hold up. So what I'm asking is for someone to come back out and repair it properly, this time at no extra charge, since it's clearly the same issue. While they're here, I'd also like to discuss the cabinet damage. You've got a good reputation and I'd happily keep recommending you, so can we get this scheduled soon?

title: Damaged And Late Delivery
prompt_text: You ordered an expensive laptop online for an important work deadline and paid extra for fast shipping. It arrived four days late, with a cracked screen and a dented case. You are now on a call with the online retailer's customer service representative. Explain both problems clearly, decide whether to ask for a replacement or a full refund and justify your choice, and ask the representative to also refund the shipping fee.
template_hint: Open by referencing the order, acknowledge that shipping issues happen, lay out the late arrival and the physical damage, explain why this was time-sensitive, weigh replacement against refund and clearly state your decision with reasons, ask for the shipping fee back, and close cooperatively.
sample_response_band12: Hi, I'm reaching out about an order I placed last week, an expensive laptop I needed for a work deadline. I paid extra for express shipping, but it arrived four days late. On top of that, when I opened the box, the screen was cracked and the case was dented. Here's where I'm at, I understand things get damaged in transit and it isn't your fault personally, but this was genuinely time-sensitive and the laptop is now unusable. After thinking it over, what I'd prefer is a full refund rather than a replacement, simply because the deadline has already passed and I've had to make other arrangements. I'd also ask you to reimburse the express shipping fee, since the package was late anyway. I think that's only fair. You've been easy to deal with, so I'm confident we can wrap this up smoothly. What's the next step on your end?

title: Broken Gym Equipment, Higher Fees
prompt_text: You have been a loyal member of your gym for three years, but several machines have been broken for weeks and the pool is closed for repairs. Despite this, the gym has just raised your monthly membership fee. Speak with a staff member at the front desk and explain your frustration. Decide whether to ask for a fee freeze until things are fixed or to negotiate cancelling your contract without a penalty, and justify your choice to them.
connector_phrases: Can I be straight with you?
It just doesn't sit right that
So I'm in a bit of a bind,
I'm hoping we can find a middle ground
template_hint: Greet the staff member and ask to speak frankly, note your long loyalty, describe the broken equipment and pool closure alongside the fee increase, explain why that combination feels unfair, choose either a fee freeze or penalty-free cancellation and justify it, and invite them to meet you in the middle.
sample_response_band12: Hi, can I be straight with you for a second? I've been a member here for three years and I really like this place, but something's been bothering me. Don't get me wrong, I know equipment breaks down, but several machines have been out of service for weeks, and the pool's been closed too. Meanwhile, you've just raised my monthly fee. It honestly doesn't sit right that I'm paying more for less. So I'm in a bit of a bind. The way I see it, the fairest thing would be to freeze my old rate until everything's actually fixed and the pool reopens. I'd much rather stay than walk away, because I value my routine here. But if that's not possible, I'd want to discuss cancelling without the usual penalty. I'm really hoping we can find a middle ground. What can you do for me?

title: Asking A Neighbour To Lower Noise
prompt_text: Your next-door neighbour has been playing loud music and hosting gatherings late into the night, and you have not been able to sleep before an important work week. You have decided to speak to them directly rather than file a complaint with the building. Talk to your neighbour, explain how the late-night noise is affecting you, and politely ask them to keep things quieter after a certain hour. Try to make your request while protecting your friendly relationship.
connector_phrases: I hate to bring this up, but
template_hint: Greet the neighbour warmly, acknowledge it is awkward, explain how the late noise affects your sleep and work, make a clear request about quiet hours with reasons, offer a flexible compromise, and reassure them you value being good neighbours.
sample_response_band12: Hey Daniel, do you have a quick second? I hate to bring this up, because honestly you've been such a great neighbour. The thing is, with these walls being so thin, the music and the get-togethers carry right into my bedroom, and the last few nights it's gone till around two. I've got a really brutal work week starting, and I just can't seem to wind down or get any sleep. I was hoping we could agree on keeping things a bit quieter after, say, eleven on weeknights? Weekends I genuinely don't mind at all, so party away then. I totally get that you like having people over, and I don't want to be that guy. Maybe just shift the late nights to Fridays? I really appreciate you hearing me out. Thanks so much, you're a star.

title: Neighbour's Tree Damaging Your Fence
prompt_text: A large tree in your neighbour's yard has grown into your fence, and its branches and roots are slowly cracking and pushing over the panels on your side. You would prefer to resolve this directly with your neighbour before involving the city or a lawyer. Speak to your neighbour, explain the damage the tree is causing, and propose a fair way to fix the fence and trim the tree without souring the relationship.
connector_phrases: I wanted to flag something with you
template_hint: Open in a friendly way, acknowledge the tree is lovely and you don't want conflict, explain the cracking damage and why it matters, propose trimming and a cost-sharing plan with reasons, offer to help arrange the work, and reassure them you want to stay on good terms.
sample_response_band12: Hi Susan, have you got a minute? I wanted to flag something with you, and I'm honestly not blaming anyone. That gorgeous maple of yours has grown a lot, and a few of the overhanging branches plus the roots are actually starting to push over my fence panels. Two of them have cracked, and I'm worried the whole section might come down. Here's what I was thinking: would you be open to having the branches trimmed back, and then maybe we split the cost of repairing the fence? It sits right on the property line, so it feels fairest that way. I'd be happy to get a couple of quotes and handle the scheduling, so it's no hassle for you. I really value being your neighbour, so let's just figure this out together calmly. Does that sound reasonable to you?

title: Addressing A Constantly Barking Dog
prompt_text: Your neighbour's dog barks loudly for hours while they are at work, and the noise carries into your home, making it hard to focus or relax. You are fond of both the dog and your neighbour, so you would rather handle this kindly than report it to the landlord. Talk to your neighbour, gently let them know about the barking they may not even be aware of, and work together toward a solution while keeping things friendly.
connector_phrases: You probably have no idea, but
template_hint: Greet warmly and compliment the dog, gently explain they likely don't know about the barking, describe how it affects you, suggest a couple of possible solutions with reasons, offer to help, and reassure them this isn't a big deal between you.
sample_response_band12: Hey Priya, can I grab you for a sec? First off, you probably have no idea, but I love Max, honestly, he's the sweetest. I just wanted to mention that while you're out at work, he barks pretty much non-stop for hours, and the sound carries straight into my place where I work from home. I think it might be a bit of separation anxiety, the poor guy. Maybe it's worth trying a chew toy or some calming treats, or even doggy daycare a couple of days a week? Do you think we could give something a try and see if it settles him? No rush at all, I just figured you'd want to know, especially for his sake. You're a great neighbour, and I really didn't want to go over your head about it. Thanks so much for understanding.

title: Neighbour Parking In Your Spot
prompt_text: A neighbour in your building keeps parking their car in your assigned parking spot, forcing you to circle the street or park far away after long days. You can either speak to them directly and ask them to stop, or report it to building management and have their car towed. Decide which option you will take, and explain your decision directly to the neighbour while trying to resolve the problem and keep the peace.
connector_phrases: I didn't want to make a big thing of this
I figured I'd talk to you first
template_hint: Approach the neighbour pleasantly, acknowledge it may be an honest mistake, explain it's your assigned spot and how it affects you, state your decision to handle it directly rather than tow, request that they stop, and reassure them you wanted to keep it neighbourly.
sample_response_band12: Hi Marco, sorry to catch you on your way in. Listen, I didn't want to make a big thing of this, so I figured I'd talk to you first instead of going to management. The thing is, spot fourteen, where you've been parking, is actually my assigned spot. I'm sure it was just an honest mix-up, but the last few nights I've had to park three blocks away after these really long shifts. Going forward, could you use your own spot or visitor parking? I'd really rather not have anyone's car towed, honestly, that's the last thing I want between neighbours. I just figured a quick chat would sort it out way more easily. Thanks a lot for understanding, Marco. I appreciate it, and hopefully that works for you. No hard feelings at all.

title: Asking To Limit Noisy Renovations
prompt_text: Your neighbour has been renovating their home for weeks, with drilling and hammering starting early in the morning and continuing on weekends. You work night shifts and need to sleep during the day, so the constant daytime noise makes it almost impossible to rest. You don't want to stop their project, but you need them to keep the loudest work to certain hours. Speak to your neighbour, explain your situation, and negotiate a schedule that lets the renovation continue while letting you rest, all without damaging the relationship.
connector_phrases: I know this is your home and
I'm in a bit of a tricky spot
I'm not asking you to stop
I really do appreciate your flexibility
template_hint: Greet the neighbour kindly and acknowledge their right to renovate, explain your night-shift situation and why daytime noise is so hard, request a limited window for the loudest work with reasons, propose a specific compromise schedule, and reassure them you support the project.
sample_response_band12: Hi Lena, do you have a moment? First, I know this is your home and you've every right to fix it up, and honestly it's going to look amazing. I'm just in a bit of a tricky spot, so I wanted to chat. I work night shifts, so I sleep during the day, and the drilling and hammering starting at seven, plus the weekend work, makes it almost impossible to rest. I'm absolutely not asking you to stop. Would there be any way to keep the really noisy stuff to, say, between ten and four on weekdays? If we could just protect the early mornings and maybe Sunday, that would honestly save me. I totally understand if the crew has set hours, so let's see what's workable. I really do appreciate your flexibility, Lena. Thank you so much.

title: Raising a Neighbour's Garbage Problem
prompt_text: Your neighbour repeatedly overfills the shared garbage and recycling bins and leaves bags piled beside them, attracting pests and creating a foul smell near your shared entrance. You can either bring it up gently with the neighbour yourself, or report it anonymously to the building's property manager. Decide which approach to take, then speak directly to the neighbour to address the problem while keeping your relationship civil.
connector_phrases: I wanted to chat with you before anything else
I get that life gets busy
Thanks for being cool about this
template_hint: Open in a respectful, non-accusatory way, acknowledge everyone gets busy, describe the overflow and the pest and smell issues, state your decision to talk directly rather than report, propose practical fixes with reasons, and reassure them you wanted to keep it friendly.
sample_response_band12: Hey Tom, have you got a second? I wanted to chat with you before anything else, rather than going to the property manager. I've noticed the shared bins by our entrance have been overflowing lately, with bags piled beside them, and honestly it's starting to smell and attract raccoons at night. I totally get that life gets busy, believe me. Could we maybe try breaking down the boxes so things fit better, and if there's ever extra, just hold it until the next pickup? I could even ask management about a second bin if we need one. It's really for everyone's sake, since we all use that doorway. I figured a quick word between us was way better than making it official. Thanks for being cool about this, Tom. I really appreciate it.

title: Children Damaging Shared Property
prompt_text: Your neighbour's young children have repeatedly damaged shared property in your complex, breaking hallway lights, denting the elevator doors, and scratching cars in the lot. You are friendly with the parents and don't want to upset them, but the damage is costing everyone money and someone could get hurt. Speak to the parents directly, raise the issue tactfully, and ask them to supervise the children more closely while preserving your good relationship.
connector_phrases: Please don't take this the wrong way
I know how energetic kids are
I really value us being friends
